campaigns.title: Help Leena in saving girls from being lost forever in red-light areas
campaigns.description: "Hundreds of young girls are lost forever in the dark lanes of red-light areas," says Leena Kejriwal, the founder of Missing Link Trust. These girls endure unimaginable abuse and exploitation, being lured or trafficked into an industry that breaks them physically and emotionally. Missing Link Trust is on a mission to prevent the tragedy before it happens, and also rehabilitate girls and women who are rescued from forced sex work.Leena’s journey began with her powerful street art project in Kolkata, where she painted haunting silhouettes of missing girls to raise awareness against sex trafficking. Now, she leads a larger fight — one that focuses on prevention and rehabilitation. But this mission needs your support.You can save countless girl children from going missing forever. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Preventing Trafficking Before It Begins“When girls come into a red-light area, the first thing is that they are broken, physically and emotionally,” Leena says. “It’s like they will be caught, hands and legs tied, and all heinous crimes committed on them.” These are the brutal realities that Missing Link Trust fights against every day.Many of these girls are from impoverished regions like the Sundarbans, and traffickers prey on their vulnerability. “It’s shocking that they sell themselves for as low as a hundred rupees,” Leena adds. "They ready her through violent acts, removing all threads of shame from her."But why wait until a girl is trafficked to save her? Missing Link Trust believes in stopping trafficking at its root — through “prevention, prevention, prevention” as Leena puts it. The goal is simple: protect girls from ever being lost to this darkness.A Safe World for Children“Our mission is the prevention of abuse and exploitation of children,” Leena emphasizes. “Our vision is to create a safe world for children, and we want to do this with a preventive methodology.”Every Donation MattersYour support will help Missing Link Trust continue its vital work of rescuing, rehabilitating, and preventing the trafficking of young girls. The funds raised will go directly towards:Prevention programs in vulnerable areas to stop trafficking before it happens.Rehabilitation services for girls rescued from red-light areas, providing them with medical care, emotional support, and education.Awareness campaigns to reach at-risk communities and ensure girls are protected from traffickers.Together, We Can Make a Difference“Why wait for a girl to get trafficked to save her?” asks Leena. With your help, we can stop this horror before it begins. Your donation will help us create a world where no girl is lost to the nightmare of trafficking.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online giving platform, to support Missing Link Trust to save young girls from being trafficked and help rehabilitate those rescued.

campaigns.title: Help provide shelter, food, clothes and medical support to abandoned widow mothers of Vrindavan. Donate now
campaigns.description: In Vrindavan, thousands of elderly widow mothers face the harsh reality of abandonment and suffering. With no one to turn to, many are left begging on the streets, exposed to heat, cold and rain,Donate so that Maitri can provide shelter, food, clothes and medical support to abandoned elderly mothers now. Give generously.Winnie Singh has witnessed many cases of widow mothers being abandoned on the streets of Vrindavan, one of the holiest places in India. “Children leave their mothers as soon as they become widows. The children start feeling the burden. This has become a practice in today's society that money comes first and everything else later,” says Winnie whose Maitri India, rescues widowed mothers from the streets of Vrindavan.Many widowed elderly mothers often end up living a lonely life and begging to survive on the streets. And Maitri aspires to take care of every widow in need of help, but because of lack of funds, Winnie and Maitri are in a position to do it."Elderly widows don't have shelter. They sleep in cowsheds over sacks. They don't have a blanket. Nights are really difficult for them," shares Winnie Singh. Maitri aims to:Provide shelter and nutritious meals leading to improved health and wellbeingMake physical and mental health services accessibleOffer necessary quality aged care and assisted living supportIncrease awareness and engagement around the struggles of abandoned widow mothersWhy donate to MaitriWinnie needs your support to help those in need of food, healthcare and well-being. “Maitri urgently needs your support to provide nutritious food, clothing and healthcare to sustain these widow mothers who are prone to more illnesses as they age,” appeals Winnie. “You can become the family they were denied. Hold their hands so they don't need to beg ever again,” she adds. Let’s rescue abandoned widows mothers from the streets of Vrindavan and give them a better future. Donate now.Our help will enable Maitri to save hundreds of abandoned widows from a life of begging, and restore the respect, dignity, and identity they deserve.

campaigns.title: Help Mansi bring hope into lives of children fighting for life, Donate now
campaigns.description: When even hospitals and doctors gave up on children fighting for life Mansi Shah stood by them. Mansi founded Happy Feet Home, an NGO that cares for children fighting severe illnesses like thalassemia, HIV, Cancer, etc. Despite being discouraged repeatedly, she refused to turn away from children who were often overlooked simply because their journeys were difficult and uncertain. Today, Happy Feet Home provides children with medical care, nutritious food, education, emotional support and, above all, the love and dignity every child deserves.Your donation can help ensure these children continue receiving lifelong care. Donate today and give them the chance to live with hope.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking.Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Born from one unforgettable momentHappy Feet Home was born from an experience Mansi could never forget. "I was very attached to this 3-year-old girl. She had a brain tumour," she says. When the child hugged her tightly before boarding a train home, it felt like a final goodbye. That heartbreaking moment inspired Mansi to dedicate her life to children who may never receive a cure. "If I start something, I will work with children who have illnesses which may not have a cure... That's when the idea of Happy Feet Home came into my mind."Every donation changes a child's futureMany children arrive at Happy Feet Home carrying far more than an illness. "It's not 1, not 2, but 3 or 4 issues that they are grappling with in life," Mansi explains. Poverty, abandonment and years without affection often leave emotional scars as deep as their medical conditions. Some children have even told her that the first hug they ever received was after arriving at the centre.Yet with proper nutrition, medicines, counselling and constant care, lives begin to change. One girl who was once not responding to treatment is now a healthy college student. Another boy arrived weighing just 17 kilos after living on the streets. Today, he is healthy and learning professional cricket. These transformations are only possible because compassionate people chose to donate.Why your support is urgently neededProviding lifelong care for children with HIV, thalassemia and cancer requires constant support. There was even a time when Happy Feet Home had to shut its centre for nearly two weeks because of a lack of funding. During that period, children lost access to nutrition, education and mental health support. As Mansi explains, "Illnesses like HIV and Thalassemia are incurable. The need for medicine, for treatment, for care and support is going to be a forever need."That is why your donation matters today.Donate to Happy Feet HomeEvery donation helps provide medicines, nutritious meals, education, counselling and a loving home for children fighting serious illnesses. As Mansi beautifully puts it, "As a donor, you are not only giving money. You are ensuring that children with illnesses have the right to live as children. You are giving them hope."If you believe no child should be denied love, dignity or happiness because of illness, please support this NGO for children today. "If you believe that children with HIV, Thalassemia, Cancer have a right to live a dignified life please support our cause and donate today."Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Happy Feet Home in its mission to provide care, dignity, medical support, and hope to children living with life-limiting and life-shortening illnesses.

campaigns.title: Don’t let children fight cancer on an empty stomach, help them with nutrition. Donate now
campaigns.description: “My 4-year-old son was diagnosed with an aggressive form of leukaemia. We brought him to Mumbai 6 months back when he was fighting for life. But within a couple of months, Vidhan’s condition improved drastically. Apart from the chemotherapy, one thing that has had a significant impact on his health is the nutrition support provided by Cuddles Foundation,” says Meenakshi Motiwala, who hails from Dhule, Maharashtra. Meenakshi and her husband had no means of supporting Vidhan with special nutrition, but thanks to the NGO, their son is in better shape.Cuddles Foundation is a nonprofit that helps hundreds of children fight cancer each year, but there are thousands more still battling cancer and in need of help. Help the NGO extend its reach and save many more lives. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India)Non-profit organisation Cuddles Foundation has been on a mission to give underprivileged children having cancer a fighting chance against the disease with complete nutritional support. Giving kids battling cancer a fighting chanceDoctors often say that proper nutrition is the first line of treatment for children fighting cancer. However, because of poor financial background, many parents can’t give their kids life-saving nutrition and often witness the condition deteriorate before their eyes. “We have observed that there is a marked improvement or maintenance in the health and nutritional status of more than 80% of children with cancer by giving them proper meals. Nutritional support can not only give a child the strength needed to go through chemotherapy, but also help in reducing the side effects of cancer treatment significantly. Sadly, the life-saving nutrition needed to help children defeat cancer is well beyond the reach for many parents due to their financial condition,” says Purnota Dutta Bahl, founder of the nonprofit. Purnota embarked upon the mission to save the lives of children with cancer over a decade back after a chance visit to the Tata Memorial Hospital. “I saw a baby girl who back then, was about the same age as my daughter. The girl was fighting cancer and was visibly malnourished. I was informed by the nurse that children get treatment for cancer at the hospital free of cost, but their condition keeps deteriorating as their parents can’t afford the required nutrition. It was on that day that I decided to at least give these children a fighting chance against cancer,” explains the founder of the nonprofit organisation. Donate to heal thousands more“We have been able to take our programme to over 50 hospitals, spread across 17 states. But still there are thousands of children we are yet to reach. The cost of providing nutritional support to a child and their family suffering from cancer is about ₹80,000 per year (or ₹7,000 a month). Cancer is absolutely curable. A little love and support from you can help children suffering from cancer complete their treatment and go on to live functional and productive lives in the future,” says Purnota.Donate to this fundraiser by give.do, India’s largest fundraising platform, to support Cuddles Foundation in their mission to give life-saving nutrition to children suffering from cancer and give them the strength to fight cancer.

campaigns.title: Help Girish give 250 children, rescued from abuse in red-light areas a new life
campaigns.description: In the dark and dingy alleys of red light areas, injustice, pain and suffering run rampant. And the worst affected are the children who grow up in red-light areas and are subjected to constant abuse and violence. “The sight of a 12-year-old girl being gang-raped by a group of men near a house l haunts me to this date. I was filled with anguish and resolved to do something,” says Girish Kulkarni who has made it his life’s mission to rescue children from red light areas and give them a safe and secure childhood. In the last 3 decades Girish has saved over 1100 girls from a life of abuse in red-light areas, giving them a loving home, education and medical care. He has further supported over 20,000 children with a better life.Sadly, Of the 250 the children rescued from abuse and exploitation, around 70 of them are HIV positive. The high cost of medication has left Girish struggling for funds. Your support can help him provide these children with the care and upbringing they need. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). A movement to protect vulnerable children“Growing up in Ahilyanagar, the experiences I had as a child witnessing the horrors of sex trade defined the path of my life. I had even seen my friend’s 15-year-old sister and 70-year-old grandmother being forced to do sex work for the price of a cup of tea”, recalls Girish. “I started by merely feeding and teaching children of sex workers initially with the intention of giving them some respite from the horrors of life of abuse in red-light areas. Gradually more and more mothers started coming to me and asked me to take their children away, even if it was just for a couple of hours,” he further adds.The work that Girish started in his youth gradually grew to become a movement, with many more joining his cause. His NGO Snehalaya to date has transformed the lives of millions. Girish’s work is crucial for the survival and safety of vulnerable children of sex workers. Snehalaya is almost entirely dependent on donations from the public and your support can strengthen his mission to save children most in need. Donate generously.The horrors of life in red light areas“The cruelty and abuse that children as young as 6 or 7 years of age face is something that I can’t explain in words. Young daughters of sex workers are made to watch their mothers being raped, are brutally assaulted and forced into sex work. No child deserves to grow like this, Girish says in anguish.Sad reality of life for children growing up in red light areas is that they are deprived of even the most basic needs like proper food, safety and education. Moreover, a good number of children rescued by Girish are diagnosed HIV positive. The task of securing medication, providing them quality education and much-needed nutrition is one that requires constant support from donors. Moreover, the increase in healthcare cost has left Girish struggling to keep his home for children going. But your support can enable him to give his children a loving environment and save many more who still remain trapped behind closed doors.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help Snehalaya give hundreds of children rescued from red-light areas a life away from violence and abuse.

campaigns.title: Help Chitra save disabled children from poor families from a life of neglect and abuse
campaigns.description: Chitra Shah noticed that a patient with disabilities had missed several of her appointments with a psychologist, where she worked. Determined to find out why, Chitra went to the girl’s house which was located near a garbage dump, and she found the house locked. But she kept hearing noises from within the house. Looking inside, she found the missing patient tied to a chair and covered in her own urine and waste. The patient was a young girl with Down’s Syndrome and because of her disability; she was locked up every day and suffered in silence. This incident led Chitra Shah to establish Satya Special School in 2003. Since then, she has provided thousands of special needs children with therapies, education and vocational skills training to live their lives with dignity and respect. Many children with disabilities are abandoned because of their disabilities. Satya Special School believes in empowering disabled children and helping them stand on their own independently so they never have to be shunned, humiliated and abused because of their disabilities. Your support can help children with disabilities receive therapy and education. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). Place of rehabilitation and transformation For 20 years, Satya Special School has been working with special-needs children in 10 centres located in rural and urban Puducherry. The centres have trained teachers and physical therapists who work with the children to identify their special needs and develop physical and educational programs to help them be integrated into mainstream life. The children are provided with multiple therapies such as physiotherapy, hydrotherapy, speech therapy, art therapy and more to improve their physical condition and help them function independently. The school also provides the children with specialized educational support to help them integrate into mainstream schools. This School Readiness Program includes one-on-one special education, activity-based learning, visual media learning and more. Satya Special School is also equipped with facilities such as a sensory walkway, an outdoor inclusive disabled-friendly play park, a wheelchair accessible vehicle and many more facilities to assist children with physical disabilities.Need for the fundraiserThe work of Satya Special School is making an impact in the lives of the children, many of whom have learned to function independently, been enrolled in mainstream schools and secured employment. But the school is in desperate need of funds to afford rehabilitation equipment such as walkers and standing frames, custom made shoes for people with disabilities and other therapeutic interventions. Your support will help the special needs children continue to have access to the tools, resources and therapies that make their lives better and give them a chance to have self-reliant and hopeful lives.The parents and guardians of the children were previously in despair regarding their children’s helplessness but because of Satya Special School, they are watching their children grow into independent and capable individuals who work towards their dreams for the future. The stigma, discrimination and abuse of disabled children is widespread, and many special-needs children are abandoned by their own families. But ”we transform their lives through building their abilities” , shares Chitra Shah, the founder of Satya Special School. Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Satya Special School in their mission to provide multiple therapies, rehabilitation, academic support and future job readiness skills to 61 special needs children.

campaigns.title: Help us Transform Village Education in Ladakh
campaigns.description: Imagine a village where learning knows no boundaries -where elders, youths and children come together to learn, share knowledge, Inspire one another, and grow as a community.Where children learn not just from books, but from the mountains, streams, farms, folksongs and the community's way of knowing and being. This is what we at village Lab Foundation are building in Ladakh - A living, breathing learning ecosystem, rooted in culture, nature, and collection wisdom.The ProblemOur VisionTo build a thriving Ladakh where Indigenous knowledge and practices are revitalised through education empowering children to carry their heritage forward with Confidence, Pride, and Purpose.﻿Our Programs𝐘𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐠 𝐑𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐘𝐑𝐏)The Young Readers Program focuses on language development and foundational literacy for children grades 2 - 5 through interactive reading and storytelling sessions. It also establishes mini-libraries across villages in Ladakh and works to create a collection of culturally relevant resources for local children.𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐋𝐚𝐝𝐚𝐤𝐡𝐢 𝐖𝐚𝐲 𝐚𝐟𝐭𝐞𝐫-𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦The Ladakhi Way After-School Program is a culturally rooted initiative designed for students in grades 6 to 9. It combines experiential learning with traditional knowledge, using the village as a learning lab and local elders and youth as facilitators. Through hands-on activities, community engagement, and creative projects, the program fosters critical thinking, communication skills, and a deep connection to Ladakh’s heritage—empowering learners to grow with confidence and purpose.WHAT WILL YOUR CONTRIBUTION EMPOWER?"I once thought I had to leave my village to find purpose. But it was in returning that I discovered it had always been here, in the mountains, the people, and the stories waiting to be held with care.”- Rigzen Wangmo, Founder-Village Lab Foundation LadakhOnce you've made your contribution, don’t stop there, share the love!Pass this campaign along to your friends, family, and kindred hearts. The more we share, the further this dream travels.Your support means the world to us. 💛Stay close to the journey, follow us on:Linkedln: Village Lab Foundation-LadakhInstagram: Village Lab Foundation-LadakhFacebook: Village Lab Foundation, Ladakhfor glimpses of the movement you’re powering.Together, let’s reimagine learning, one village, one child, one act of love at a time #TheLadakhiWay

campaigns.title: Support the Treatment of Children Suffering from Blood Cancer (Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia)
campaigns.description:
In 2018 alone, 9.6 Million people worldwide are estimated to die from cancer. However, 30%-35% of all cancer can be prevented (World Health Organisation).The World Health Organization (WHO) defines cancer as “the uncontrolled growth and spread of cells”. It is the second leading cause of death worldwide. India has the third highest number of cancer cases in the world, after China and the US.Childhood Leukemia (commonly known as blood cancer) is the most common form of cancer amongst children, accounting for approximately 32% of all cancer cases in children younger than 15 years of age. Acute lymphoblastic leukemia is the most common form of childhood cancer- accounting for approximately 75% of all cases of childhood leukemia.According to the Huffington Post, only 1 in 10 of childhood cancer cases reported in India receive complete therapy. The cure rates for common childhood cancers are 80% in developed countries, however in India they have remained abysmally low at around 30%.Each one of us can make a difference by sponsoring a child’s treatment or enabling their access to supportive service. 
 The Yuvraj Singh Foundation has created a targeted programme aimed at supporting the treatment of children suffering from acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Through the programme, we support beneficiaries from marginalized and underprivileged backgrounds. One of the key features of this programme is to create a well-planned treatment pathway, encompassing- diagnostic tests, investigations, evidence based second opinion, chemotherapy, supportive care, and counseling and post treatment support.  
 The programme will provide financial support for the treatment of 25 children suffering from acute lymphoblastic leukemia. This will be done by providing holistic treatment and care to the identified cancer patients, at eminent cancer hospitals which have the three following specializations: medical oncology, surgical oncology and radiation oncology. The financial support provided will include - cost of diagnostic tests, investigations, evidence based second opinion, chemotherapy, supportive care and medicines. One of the unique features of the programme is its holistic nature and thus as a process counseling and post treatment support to cancer patients and their caregivers will be provided by certified counselors.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.

campaigns.description: I am fundraising for a cause close to my heart, to bring a lasting change in the lives of the underprivileged.
